event_log.detail:
What: Britain's Calder Hall became the world's first nuclear power station to deliver electricity on an industrial scale.
Why it matters: It moved nuclear power from laboratory demonstration to genuine commercial output.
Why you should care: It began the fleet of large reactors that today supply roughly a tenth of the world's electricity.
(Source: World Nuclear Association)
event_log.title: ⚡ Today's spark — Calder Hall, first industrial-scale nuclear (1956)

event_log.detail:
On this day
1935: American physician Bob Smith had his last alcoholic drink, marking the traditional founding date of Alcoholics Anonymous.

Featured: Amalthea (mythology)
In Greek mythology, Amalthea or Amaltheia is the figure most commonly identified as the nurse of Zeus during his infancy. She is described either as a nymph who raises the child on the milk of a goat or, in some accounts from the Hellenistic period onwards, as the goat itself.

Money basics
The Rule of 72: divide 72 by an annual interest rate to estimate how many years money takes to double - at 8% growth, savings double roughly every 9 years. (Source: Investor.gov)

What looks as if it is going to swallow the great Pillars of Creation? The Eagle Nebula (M16) is not a bird, a plane, or Superman. M16 is actually a combination of several celestial objects. NGC 6611 is the young star cluster that appears to peak out beneath the Eagle’s “wings”...
(c) Emmanuel Delgadillo Text: Keighley Rockcliffe (NASA GSFC, UMBC CSST, CRESST II)

event_log.detail:
What: Perovskite materials, often layered on top of silicon, keep setting new records for how much sunlight a cell can convert.
Why it matters: Stacking two materials captures more of the spectrum, pushing efficiency past what silicon alone can reach.
Why you should care: Higher-efficiency panels mean more power from the same roof — and cheaper solar for everyone.
(Source: National Renewable Energy Laboratory)
event_log.title: ⚡ Today's spark — The next generation of solar cells

event_log.detail: The Great Pyramid of Giza, finished around 2560 BCE from some 2.3 million stone blocks, stood as the tallest human structure on Earth for nearly 3,800 years. Archaeology has overturned the slave-labor myth: excavated workers' villages with bakeries, breweries, and medical care show it was built by rotating crews of paid, well-fed Egyptian laborers. Its base is level to within a couple of centimeters.
event_log.title: The 4,500-Year-Old Skyscraper

1999: In the aftermath of the bombing of Yugoslavia and the Kosovo War, the NATO-led Kosovo Force entered the territory with a mandate of establishing a secure environment.

Featured: Love Is Embarrassing
"Love Is Embarrassing" is a song by American singer-songwriter Olivia Rodrigo from her second studio album, Guts (2023). Rodrigo wrote the song with its producer, Dan Nigro. It became available as the album's ninth track on September 8, 2023, when it was released by Geffen...

event_log.title: Daily learning

event_log.detail:
What: The Itaipu Dam on the Paraná River is jointly owned and operated by Brazil and Paraguay, with about 14,000 megawatts of capacity.
Why it matters: It's a rare bi-national power plant, splitting both the electricity and the governance between two countries.
Why you should care: It supplies most of Paraguay's power and a big share of Brazil's — proof that shared energy projects can work.
(Source: Itaipu Binacional)
event_log.title: ⚡ Today's spark — Itaipu — a dam shared by two nations
